content:
Probably not the whole PMS.

2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

When people talk about Airbnb automation, they often jump straight to big systems: PMS, channel management, pricing tools, cleaner coordination, smart locks, and dashboards.
But for small self-managing hosts, I think the first AI workflow should be much closer to the guest.
Guests feel slow replies, unclear check-in details, parking uncertainty, late-arrival questions, and vague house rules before they care about your back-office stack.
That is why I’ve been thinking about guest communication as the first practical AI layer for small hosts.
Not “AI replies to everything.”
More like:

• answer routine questions with real house knowledge

• draft uncertain replies instead of guessing

• ask the host before refunds, access issues, cleaning uncertainty, complaints, or policy exceptions

• turn repeated guest questions into better listing guidance
The interesting part is that guest messages are not just support work. They also reveal what future guests may hesitate about before booking: parking, workspace, late arrival, family setup, heating, local food, or house rules.
So the first useful AI layer may not be full automation. It may be a lightweight system that helps a host answer better, stay in control, and improve the listing over time.
I wrote the longer practical guide here:
https://www.morphicai.io/blog/airbnb-automation-for-small-hosts
